briefdescription.para: Custom graph marquee.

detaileddescription.para: Requires an existing marquee and a graph as current top window

briefdescription.para: Return the logbook type, one of LogbookTypes.
Return the logbook waves.
Extract a date/time slice of the logbook wave.
Extract the delta time slice of the logbook wave.
Extract the sweep number slice of the labnotebook values wave.
Extract a slice of the logbook wave and makes it empty.

memberdef.argsstring: (wave logbook, variable logbookType, variable colOrLayer, string suffix)
memberdef.name: ExtractLogbookSlice

briefdescription.para: Extract a single column/layer of the labnotebook/TPStorage values wave.

detaileddescription.para: This is useful if you want to plot values against e.g time and let Igor do the formatting of the date/time values. Always returns a numerical wave.

briefdescription.para: Return a list of the AD channels from the DAQ config.
Return a list of the DA channels from the DAQ config.
Return a list of the TTL channels from the DAQ config.
Return a wave with all active channels.

xrefdescription.para: change to return a 0/1 wave with constant size a la DAG_GetChannelState

parameterdescription.para: DAQConfigWave as passed to the ITC XOP

briefdescription.para: Returns the number of given mode channels from channelType wave.

parameterdescription.para: a 1D wave containing DaqChannelTypeConstants, returned by GetADCTypesFromConfig()

simplesect.para: number of types present in chanTypes

briefdescription.para: Return a types of the AD channels from the ITC config.
Return a types of the AD channels from the ITC config.
Return a types of the DA channels from the ITC config.
Return a wave with all active channels.

xrefdescription.para: change to return a 0/1 wave with constant size a la DAG_GetChannelState

parameterdescription.para: DAQConfigWave as passed to the ITC XOP

briefdescription.para: Checks if a channel of TP type exists on ADCs.

simplesect.para: 1 if TP type present, 0 otherwise

briefdescription.para: Return the dimension label for the special, aka non-unique, controls.
Returns the name of a control from the DA_EPHYS panel.

detaileddescription.para: Constants are defined at ChannelTypeAndControlConstants

memberdef.argsstring: (wave wv, variable col, variable val, variable forwardORBackward, variable entrySourceType, variable *first, variable *last)
memberdef.name: FindRange

briefdescription.para: Find the first and last point index of a consecutive range of values in the labnotebook.

parameterdescription.para: find the first(1) or last(0) range
type of the labnotebook entry, one of DataAcqModes
point index of the beginning of the range
point index of the end of the range

briefdescription.para: Test helper to enforce that every query done for an INDEP_HEADSTAGE setting does not search an entry which is HEADSTAGE dependent. The additional check that not all entries are the same is for really old legacy entries which are INDEP but set for all headstages.

detaileddescription.para: Does nothing outside of CI.

briefdescription.para: Returns the numerical index for the sweep number column in the settings history waves (numeric and text)

memberdef.argsstring: (wave numericalValues, variable sweepNo, string setting, variable entrySourceType, variable defValue=defaultValue)
memberdef.name: GetLastSettingIndep

briefdescription.para: Return a headstage independent setting from the numerical labnotebook.

simplesect.para: the headstage independent setting or defValue

memberdef.argsstring: (WaveText textualValues, variable sweepNo, string setting, variable entrySourceType, string defValue=defaultValue)
memberdef.name: GetLastSettingTextIndep

briefdescription.para: Return a headstage independent setting from the textual labnotebook.

simplesect.para: the headstage independent setting or defValue

memberdef.argsstring: (wave numericalValues, variable sweepNo, string setting, variable entrySourceType, variable defValue=defaultValue)
memberdef.name: GetLastSettingIndepRAC

briefdescription.para: Return a headstage independent setting from the numerical labnotebook of the sweeps in the same RA cycle.

simplesect.para: the headstage independent setting or defValue

memberdef.argsstring: (wave numericalValues, variable sweepNo, string setting, variable headstage, variable entrySourceType, variable defValue=defaultValue)
memberdef.name: GetLastSettingIndepSCI

briefdescription.para: Return a headstage independent setting from the numerical labnotebook of the sweeps in the same RA cycle.

simplesect.para: the headstage independent setting or defValue

memberdef.argsstring: (wave numericalValues, WaveText textualValues, variable sweepNo, string setting, variable entrySourceType, string defValue=defaultValue)
memberdef.name: GetLastSettingTextIndepRAC

briefdescription.para: Return a headstage independent setting from the numerical labnotebook of the sweeps in the same RA cycle.

simplesect.para: the headstage independent setting or defValue

memberdef.argsstring: (wave numericalValues, WaveTextOrNull textualValues, variable sweepNo, string setting, variable channelNumber, variable channelType, variable entrySourceType)
memberdef.name: GetLastSettingChannel

briefdescription.para: Return a numerical or text setting for the given channel.

detaileddescription.para: It also returns headstage independent entries when the given channel refers to an active channel.

simplesect.para: the wave containing the setting and the index into it.

memberdef.argsstring: (wave numericalValues, wave values, variable sweepNo, string setting, variable channelNumber, variable channelType, variable entrySourceType)
memberdef.name: GetLastSettingChannelInternal

briefdescription.para: Return a numerical/textual setting for the given channel.

detaileddescription.para: The function takes care of associated/unassociated channel properties and all other internals.

parameterdescription.para: labnotebook to read data from, either numerical or textual
name of the labnotebook entry to search
type of the labnotebook entry, one of DataAcqModes. If you don't care about the entry source type pass UNKNOWN_MODE.

simplesect.para: A tuple of the result wave and the index into it.

memberdef.argsstring: (wave values, variable sweepNo, string setting, variable entrySourceType)
memberdef.name: GetLastSetting

briefdescription.para: Return a numeric/textual wave with the latest value of a setting from the numerical/labnotebook labnotebook for the given sweep number.

parameterdescription.para: name of the setting to query
type of the labnotebook entry, one of DataAcqModes. If you don't care about the entry source type pass UNKNOWN_MODE.

simplesect.para: a free wave with LABNOTEBOOK_LAYER_COUNT rows. In case the setting could not be found an invalid wave reference is returned.

memberdef.argsstring: (wave values, variable sweepNo, string setting, variable entrySourceType, variable *first=defaultValue, variable *last=defaultValue, variable *rowIndex=defaultValue)
memberdef.name: GetLastSettingNoCache

briefdescription.para: Return a wave with the latest value of a setting from the numerical/textual labnotebook for the given sweep number. Uncached version, general users should prefer GetLastSetting().

parameterdescription.para: name of the setting to query
type of the labnotebook entry, one of DataAcqModes

[optional] Can be used to query and return the labnotebook row range. Useful for routines which must make a lot of queries to the same sweep and want to avoid the overhead of calculating first and last. Passing LABNOTEBOOK_GET_RANGE will set the calculated values of first and last after the function returns. Passing a value greater or equal zero will use these values instead.
[optional] return the row where the setting could be found, otherwise it is set to LABNOTEBOOK_MISSING_VALUE

simplesect.para: a free wave with LABNOTEBOOK_LAYER_COUNT rows. In case the setting could not be found an invalid wave reference is returned.

memberdef.argsstring: (wave numericalValues, WaveText textualValues, variable sweepNo, string setting, variable entrySourceType)
memberdef.name: GetLastSettingTextRAC

briefdescription.para: Return the last textual value of the sweeps in the same RA cycle.

memberdef.argsstring: (wave numericalValues, variable sweepNo, string setting, variable entrySourceType)
memberdef.name: GetLastSettingRAC

briefdescription.para: Return the last numerical value of the sweeps in the same RA cycle.

memberdef.argsstring: (wave numericalValues, variable sweepNo, string setting, variable entrySourceType, variable defValue=defaultValue)
memberdef.name: GetLastSettingIndepEachRAC

briefdescription.para: Return the last numerical value for the given setting of each sweep in the same RA cycle.

detaileddescription.para: The returned wave will have NaN for sweeps which do not have that entry. This is done in order to keep the indizes intact.

memberdef.argsstring: (wave numericalValues, WaveText textualValues, variable sweepNo, string setting, variable entrySourceType, string defValue=defaultValue)
memberdef.name: GetLastSettingTextIndepEachRAC

briefdescription.para: Return the last textual value for the given setting of each sweep in the same RA cycle.

detaileddescription.para: The returned wave will have NaN for sweeps which do not have that entry. This is done in order to keep the indizes intact.

memberdef.argsstring: (wave numericalValues, variable sweepNo, string setting, variable headstage, variable entrySourceType)
memberdef.name: GetLastSettingEachRAC

briefdescription.para: Return the last numerical value for the given setting of each sweep for a given headstage in the same RA cycle.

detaileddescription.para: The returned wave will have NaN for sweeps which do not have that entry. This is done in order to keep the indizes intact.

memberdef.argsstring: (wave numericalValues, WaveText textualValues, variable sweepNo, string setting, variable headstage, variable entrySourceType)
memberdef.name: GetLastSettingTextEachRAC

briefdescription.para: Return the last textual value for the given setting of each sweep for a given headstage in the same RA cycle.

detaileddescription.para: The returned wave will have NaN for sweeps which do not have that entry. This is done in order to keep the indizes intact.

memberdef.argsstring: (wave numericalValues, WaveText textualValues, variable sweepNo, string setting, variable headstage, variable entrySourceType)

briefdescription.para: Return the last textual value of the sweeps in the same stimset cycle.

memberdef.argsstring: (wave numericalValues, variable sweepNo, string setting, variable headstage, variable entrySourceType)
memberdef.name: GetLastSettingSCI

briefdescription.para: Return the last numerical value of the sweeps in the same stimset cycle.

memberdef.argsstring: (wave numericalValues, variable sweepNo, string setting, variable headstage, variable entrySourceType, variable defValue=defaultValue)
memberdef.name: GetLastSettingIndepEachSCI

briefdescription.para: Return the last numerical value for the given setting of each sweep in the same stimset cycle.

detaileddescription.para: The returned wave will have NaN for sweeps which do not have that entry. This is done in order to keep the indizes intact.

memberdef.argsstring: (wave numericalValues, WaveText textualValues, variable sweepNo, variable headstage, string setting, variable entrySourceType, string defValue=defaultValue)
memberdef.name: GetLastSettingTextIndepEachSCI

briefdescription.para: Return the last textual value for the given setting of each sweep in the same stimset cycle.

detaileddescription.para: The returned wave will have NaN for sweeps which do not have that entry. This is done in order to keep the indizes intact.

memberdef.argsstring: (wave numericalValues, variable sweepNo, string setting, variable headstage, variable entrySourceType)
memberdef.name: GetLastSettingEachSCI

briefdescription.para: Return the last numerical value for the given setting of each sweep for a given headstage in the same stimset cycle.

detaileddescription.para: The returned wave will have NaN for sweeps which do not have that entry. This is done in order to keep the indizes intact.

memberdef.name: GetLastSettingTextEachSCI

briefdescription.para: Return the last textual value for the given setting of each sweep for a given headstage in the same stimset cycle.

detaileddescription.para: The returned wave will have NaN for sweeps which do not have that entry. This is done in order to keep the indizes intact.

briefdescription.para: Return a wave with all labnotebook rows which have a non-empty entry for setting.
Return a wave with all sweep numbers which have a non-empty entry for setting.

parameterdescription.para: name of the value to search

simplesect.para: a 1D free wave with the matching sweep numbers. In case the setting could not be found an invalid wave reference is returned.

briefdescription.para: Return the last numerical value of a setting from the labnotebook and the sweep it was set.

parameterdescription.para: name of the value to search
sweep number the value was last set

simplesect.para: a free wave with LABNOTEBOOK_LAYER_COUNT rows. In case the setting could not be found an invalid wave reference is returned.

briefdescription.para: Return the last numerical value of a headstage independent setting from the labnotebook and the sweep it was set.

sweep number the value was last set
[optional, defaults to NaN] value to return in case nothing could be found

briefdescription.para: Return the last textual value of a setting from the labnotebook and the sweep it was set.

parameterdescription.para: name of the value to search
sweep number the value was last set

simplesect.para: a free wave with LABNOTEBOOK_LAYER_COUNT rows. In case the setting could not be found an invalid wave reference is returned.

memberdef.argsstring: (wave numericalValues, string setting, variable *sweepNo, string defValue=defaultValue)
memberdef.name: GetLastSweepWithSettingTextI

briefdescription.para: Return the last textual value of a headstage independent setting from the labnotebook and the sweep it was set.

parameterdescription.para: name of the value to search
sweep number the value was last set
[optional, defaults to an empty string] value to return in case nothing could be found

briefdescription.para: Returns a list of all devices, e.g. "ITC18USB_Dev_0;..." which were locked at some point.
Returns a list of all devices, e.g. "ITC18USB_Dev_0;", which have content.

parameterdescription.para: [optional, defaults to CONTENT_TYPE_SWEEP] type of content to look for, one of CONTENT_TYPES

briefdescription.para: Convenience wrapper for KillOrMoveToTrashPath()
Delete a datafolder or wave. If this is not possible, because Igor has locked the file, the wave or datafolder is moved into a trash folder named root:mies:trash_$digit.

detaileddescription.para: The trash folders will be removed, if possible, from KillTemporaries().

parameterdescription.para: absolute path to a datafolder or wave

briefdescription.para: Return a wave reference wave with all single column waves of the given channel type.

detaileddescription.para: Holds invalid wave refs for non-existing entries.

simplesect.para: GetDAQDataSingleColumnWave() or SplitSweepIntoComponents()

memberdef.argsstring: (dfref sweepDFR, variable channelType, variable channelNumber, variable splitTTLBits=defaultValue, variable ttlBit=defaultValue)
memberdef.name: GetDAQDataSingleColumnWave

briefdescription.para: Return a 1D data wave previously created by SplitSweepIntoComponents()

detaileddescription.para: Returned wave reference can be invalid.

parameterdescription.para: [optional, defaults to false] return a single bit of the TTL wave

briefdescription.para: Check if the given sweep number is valid.
Check if the given epoch number is valid.
Returns the config wave for a given sweep wave.
Returns the, possibly non existing, sweep data wave for the given sweep number.
Return the config wave name.
Return the sweep wave name.
Returns the sampling interval of the sweep in microseconds (1e-6s)
Returns the data offset of the sweep in points.
Write the given property to the config wave.

simplesect.para: Please add new properties as required

briefdescription.para: Return the hardware type of the device.

simplesect.para: One of HardwareDACTypeConstants

briefdescription.para: Parse a device string: for ITC devices of the form X_DEV_Y, where X is from DEVICE_TYPES_ITC and Y from DEVICE_NUMBERS. for NI devices of the form X, where X is from DAP_GetNIDeviceList()

detaileddescription.para:
Returns the result in deviceType and deviceNumber. Currently the parsing is successfull if for ITC devices X and Y are non-empty. for NI devices X is non-empty. deviceNumber is empty for NI devices as it does not apply
device
input device string X_DEV_Y
deviceType
returns the device type X
deviceNumber
returns the device number Y
one on successfull parsing, zero on error

briefdescription.para: Layout the DataBrowser/SweepBrowser graph.

detaileddescription.para: Takes also care of adding free axis for the headstage display.

Concept:
Block [#]: One axis with surrounded GRAPH_DIV_SPACING space
Slot [#]: Unit of vertical space, a block can occupy multiple slots
We have 100% space for all axes
AD axes should occupy four times the space of DA/TTL channels
So DA/TTL occupy one slot, AD occupy four slots
Between each axes we want GRAPH_DIV_SPACING clear space
Count the number of vertical blocks and slots to be used
Derive the space per slot
For overlay channels we reserve only one slot times slot multiplier per channel

The display order from top to bottom:
Associated channels (above: DA, below: AD) with increasing headstage number
Unassociated channels (above: DA, below: AD)
TTL channels
For overlayed channels we have up to three blocks (DA, AD, TTL) in that order.

memberdef.argsstring: (string graph, TiledGraphSettings *tgs, WaveText allVerticalAxes, WaveText allHorizontalAxes)
memberdef.name: TweakAxes

memberdef.argsstring: (string graph, WaveText axes, variable spacePerSlot, variable *first, variable *last)
memberdef.name: EnableAxis

briefdescription.para: Helper function for LayoutGraph()

detaileddescription.para: Enables the given axis between [last - spacePerSlot, last] and updates both on return. Expects last to be 1.0 on the first call.

briefdescription.para: Helper function for CreateTiledChannelGraph and friends.

detaileddescription.para: Return the next trace index for a graph which uses our trace data storage wave.

memberdef.argsstring: (string graph, wave config, variable sweepNo, wave numericalValues, WaveText textualValues, TiledGraphSettings *tgs, dfref sweepDFR, WaveText axisLabelCache, variable *traceIndex, string experiment, wave channelSelWave, BufferedDrawInfo *bdi=defaultValue)
memberdef.name: CreateTiledChannelGraph

briefdescription.para: Create a vertically tiled graph for displaying AD and DA channels.

detaileddescription.para: For preservering the axis scaling callers should do the following: embed:rst:leading-asterisk * .. code-block:: igorpro * * WAVE ranges = GetAxesRanges(graph) * * CreateTiledChannelGraph() * * SetAxesRanges(graph, ranges) *

parameterdescription.para: settings for tuning the display, see TiledGraphSettings
top datafolder to splitted 1D sweep waves
[internal use only] set to zero on the first call in a row of successive calls
name of the experiment the sweep stems from
[optional, default = n/a] initialized BufferedDrawInfo structure, when given draw calls are buffered instead for later execution

simplesect.para: GetSweepSettingsTextKeyWave for the format we need points here with taking the onset delays into account

briefdescription.para: Return a trace name prefix suitable for GetNextTraceIndex()
Runs through all graph groups in the json and appends them to the graph.

memberdef.argsstring: (string w, string v, string h, variable r, variable g, variable b, wave y, WaveText t, WaveRefWave d)
memberdef.name: TiledGraphAccelerateAppendTracesImpl

briefdescription.para: Appends a group of traces to a graph, properties w to b must be constant for the group.

parameterdescription.para: 1D wave with indices into wave d for the actual plot data
1D wave with trace names, same size as y
wave reference wave with plot data

memberdef.argsstring: (variable headstage, string channelType=defaultValue, variable activeChannelCount=defaultValue, variable channelSubNumber=defaultValue)
memberdef.name: GetHeadstageColor

briefdescription.para: Return the color of the given headstage.

parameterdescription.para: Headstage, can be NaN for non-associated channels
[optional, empty by default] The channel type for non-associated channels, currently only "TTL" is supported
[optional, empty by default] For plotting "TTL" channels only
[optional, empty by default] For plotting "TTL" channels only, "TTL Bit" information when plotting each on its own

memberdef.argsstring: (string graph, string axisRegExp=defaultValue, variable axisOffset=defaultValue, variable axisOrientation=defaultValue, variable sortOrder=defaultValue, string listForBegin=defaultValue, string listForEnd=defaultValue)
memberdef.name: EquallySpaceAxis

briefdescription.para: Space the matching axis in an equal manner.

parameterdescription.para: [optional, defaults to ".*"] regular expression matching the axes names
[optional, defaults to 0] offset of first axis in parts of total width
[optional, defaults to all] allows to apply equalization to all axis of one orientation
[optional, defaults to no sorting (NaN)] apply different sorting schemes to list of axes, see sortingOrder parameter of SortList
[optional, defaults to an empty list] list of axes to move to the front of the sorted axis list
[optional, defaults to an empty list] list of axes to move to the end of the sorted axis list

memberdef.argsstring: (string graph, string allAxes, string distAxes, variable axisOffset=defaultValue)
memberdef.name: EquallySpaceAxisPA

briefdescription.para: This is a light weight adapted version of.

simplesect.para: EquallySpaceAxis It allows to give a list of distAxes that do not require to exist. Non-existing axes are taken into account on the distribution, but are skipped when the graph is accessed. Also removing images from a graph does not update AxisList until the graph is updated, so we can not rely on Axislist here as we do the Layout after pending changes So with allAxes = "1;2;3;4;" and distAxes = "2;3;" axis 2 and 3 are set at 25% to 50% and 50% to 75% respectively.

parameterdescription.para: Name of graph where axes from distAxes list exist
List of all axes, that should be distributed. May include non-existing axes.
List of axes that are distributed. Only axes that appear in allAxes as well are modified.
[optional, default = 0] offset between 0 and 1 where distribution starts.

briefdescription.para: Save the current experiment under a new name and clear all/some data.

parameterdescription.para: mode for generating the experiment name, one of SaveExperimentModes

briefdescription.para: Cleanup the experiment name.
Return the maximum count of the given type.
Extract an one dimensional wave from the given sweep/hardware data wave and column.

parameterdescription.para: sweep wave or hardware data wave from all hardware types
index into sweep, can be queried with AFH_GetDAQDataColumn

simplesect.para: a reference to a free wave with the single channel data

briefdescription.para: Perform common transformations on the graphs traces.

detaileddescription.para: Keeps track of all internal details wrt. to the order of the operations, backups, etc.
Needs to be called after adding/removing/updating sweeps via AddSweepToGraph(), RemoveSweepFromGraph(), UpdateSweepInGraph().

parameterdescription.para:
[optional, defaults to invalid wave reference] additional data for subsequent users. Currently supported:
POST_PLOT_REMOVED_SWEEPS -> OVS indizes of the removed sweep
POST_PLOT_ADDED_SWEEPS -> OVS indizes of the added sweep Use OVS_GetSweepAndExperiment() to convert an index into a sweep/experiment pair.

briefdescription.para: Time Alignment for the BrowserSettingsPanel.

detaileddescription.para: This function should work for any given reference trace in pps.timeAlignRefTrace in the popup menu. (DB and SB)

briefdescription.para: return a list of all traces relevant for TimeAlignment
Adds or removes the cursors from the graphs depending on the panel settings.

parameterdescription.para: main DB/SB graph or any subwindow panel.

briefdescription.para: Enable/Disable TimeAlignment Controls and Cursors.

memberdef.argsstring: (string graphs, variable region=defaultValue, variable channelType=defaultValue, variable prefixTraces=defaultValue)
memberdef.name: GetAllSweepTraces

briefdescription.para: Get a textwave of all traces from a list of graphs.

parameterdescription.para: semicolon separated list of graph names
[optional] return only traces with the specified region userdata entry
[optional] return only the traces with the given channel type
[optional, defaults to true] prefix the traces names with the graph name and a #

simplesect.para: graph::trace named patterns

memberdef.argsstring: (string graph, string key, variable channelType=defaultValue, variable region=defaultValue)
memberdef.name: GetSweepUserData

memberdef.argsstring: (string graph, variable averagingEnabled, dfref averageDataFolder, variable hideSweep)
memberdef.name: AverageWavesFromSameYAxisIfReq

briefdescription.para: Average traces in the graph from the same y-axis and append them to the graph.

parameterdescription.para: graph with traces create by CreateTiledChannelGraph
switch if averaging is enabled or not
permanent datafolder where the average waves can be stored
are normal channel traces hidden or not

memberdef.argsstring: (WaveRefWave waveRefs, dfref averageDataFolder, string averageWaveName, variable skipCRC=defaultValue, variable writeSourcePaths=defaultValue, wave inputAverage=defaultValue)
memberdef.name: CalculateAverage

briefdescription.para: Calculate the average of a list of waves, wrapper for MIES_fWaveAverage().

detaileddescription.para:
For performance enhancements:
The average waves are cached
References to existing average waves are returned in case they already exist

parameterdescription.para: waves to average in a wave reference wave
folder where the data is to be stored
base name of the averaged data
[optional, defaults to false] Add the average wave CRC as suffix to its name
[optional, defaults to true] Write the full paths of the source waves into the average wave note

[optional, defaults to invalid wave ref] Override the average calculation and use the given wave as result. This is relevant for callers which want to leverage MultiThread statements together with MIES_fWaveAverage.

simplesect.para: wave reference to the average wave

briefdescription.para: Converts a free wave to a permanent wave with Overwrite.

parameterdescription.para: wave that should be converted to a permanent wave
data folder where permanent wave is stored
name of permanent wave that is created

simplesect.para: wave reference to the permanent wave

briefdescription.para: Zero all given traces.

memberdef.argsstring: (string graphtrace, variable mode, variable level, variable pos1x, variable pos2x, variable force=defaultValue)
memberdef.name: TimeAlignmentIfReq

briefdescription.para: Perform time alignment of features in the sweep traces.

parameterdescription.para: reference trace in the form of graph::trace
level input to the FindLevel operation in
specify start range for feature position
specify end range for feature position
[optional, defaults to false] redo time aligment regardless of wave note

memberdef.argsstring: (wave wv, variable mode, variable level, variable first, variable last)
memberdef.name: CalculateFeatureLoc

briefdescription.para: Find the given feature in the given wave range first and last are in x coordinates and clipped to valid values.

memberdef.argsstring: (string graph, variable ignoreAxesWithLevelCrossing=defaultValue, variable level=defaultValue, variable rangePerClampMode=defaultValue)
memberdef.name: EqualizeVerticalAxesRanges

briefdescription.para: Equalize all vertical axes ranges so that they cover the same range.

parameterdescription.para: [optional, defaults to false] ignore all vertical axis which cross the given level in the visible range
[optional, defaults to zero] level to be used for ignoreAxesWithLevelCrossing=1
[optional, defaults to false] use separate Y ranges per clamp mode

briefdescription.para: Extract the sweep number from a $something_* string.
Return the list of unlocked DA_Ephys panels.
Return the list of locked devices.
Return the list of locked ITC1600 devices.
Return the type, CHANNEL_TYPE_DAC, CHANNEL_TYPE_TTL or CHANNEL_TYPE_UNKNOWN, of the stimset.

detaileddescription.para: All callers must ensure that they can handle the unexpected CHANNEL_TYPE_UNKNOWN properly.

briefdescription.para: Return the stimset folder from the numeric channelType, CHANNEL_TYPE_DAC or CHANNEL_TYPE_TTL.

simplesect.para: Data Folder reference to Stimset dataFolder

briefdescription.para: Return the stimset folder from the numeric channelType, CHANNEL_TYPE_DAC or CHANNEL_TYPE_TTL.

simplesect.para: String with full path to Stimset dataFolder

briefdescription.para: Get the stimset parameter folder.

simplesect.para: dataFolder as DFREF

briefdescription.para: Get the stimset parameter folder.

simplesect.para: dataFolder as String

briefdescription.para: Get the TTL bit mask from the labnotebook.
Return a wave with the used TTL channels/bits which are indexed by DAEphys TTL channels.
Return a wave with the used TTL channels which are indexed by DAEphys TTL channels.

detaileddescription.para: The returned wave is not hardware independent. For ITC hardware this will only ever return at most one active channel.

memberdef.argsstring: (wave numericalValues, wave textualValues, variable sweepNo, variable channelType, variable TTLmode=defaultValue)
memberdef.name: GetActiveChannels

briefdescription.para: Return a fixed size wave with the the active channels for the given channel type.

detaileddescription.para: The function takes into account unassociated DA/AD channels as well. It returns fixed size waves with the active entries having the same value as their index. This allows users to either remove unused entries and use the wave as active entries list or use the whole wave where not being NaN is active.
With the following DAEphys setup (only the first four channels are shown)

para.verbatim: embed:rst:leading-asterisk * * +----+----+----+-----+ * | Nr | DA | AD | TTL | * +====+====+====+=====+ * | 0 | | • | | * +----+----+----+-----+ * | 1 | • | | | * +----+----+----+-----+ * | 2 | | | • | * +----+----+----+-----+ * | 3 | | | • | * +----+----+----+-----+ * * this function returns the following: * * - ``DA``: ``{NaN, 1, NaN, NaN, ...}`` * - ``AD``: ``{0, NaN, NaN, NaN, ...}`` * - ``TTL``: * * - NI hardware (regardless of TTLmode): ``{NaN, NaN, 2, 3, ...}`` * - ITC hardware (ITC18 specifically) with TTLmode: * * - ``TTL_DAEPHYS_CHANNEL``: ``{NaN, NaN, 2, 3, ...}`` * - ``TTL_HARDWARE_CHANNEL``: ``{NaN, 1, NaN, NaN, ...}`` * *

parameterdescription.para: [optional, defaults to TTL_DAEPHYS_CHANNEL] One of ActiveChannelsTTLMode. Does only apply to TTL channels.

briefdescription.para: Return the index for headstage independent data.

detaileddescription.para: Before dfe2d862 (Make the function AB_SplitTTLWaveIntoComponents available for all, 2015-10-07) we stored headstage independent data in either all entries or only the first one. Since that commit we store the data in INDEP_HEADSTAGE.

briefdescription.para: Return a list of TTL stimsets which are indexed by DAEphys TTL channels.

detaileddescription.para: The indexing here is hardware independent. For ITC hardware the assertion "log(ttlBit)/log(2) == DAEphys TTL channel" holds.

briefdescription.para: Return the name short String of the Parameter Wave used in the WaveBuilder.

simplesect.para: name as string

briefdescription.para: Returns the mode of all setVars in the DA_Ephys panel of a controlType.
Returns the mode of all setVars in the DA_Ephys panel of a controlType.
Returns the index of all popupmenus in the DA_Ephys panel of a controlType.
Returns the string contents of all popupmenus in the DA_Ephys panel of a controlType.
Extract the analysis function name from the wave note of the stim set.

simplesect.para: Analysis function for the given event type, empty string if none is set

briefdescription.para: Return the analysis function parameters as comma (,) separated list.

simplesect.para: GetWaveBuilderWaveTextParam() for the exact format.

memberdef.argsstring: (wave data, variable ttlBits, dfref targetDFR, string wavePrefix, variable rescale)
memberdef.name: SplitTTLWaveIntoComponents

briefdescription.para: Split TTL data into a single wave for each bit.

detaileddescription.para: This function is only for data from ITC hardware.

data
1D channel data extracted by ExtractOneDimDataFromSweep
ttlBits
bit mask of the active TTL channels form e.g. GetTTLBits
targetDFR
datafolder where to put the waves, can be a free datafolder
wavePrefix
prefix of the created wave names
rescale
One of TTLRescalingOptions. Rescales the data to be in the range [0, 1] when on, does no rescaling when off.
The created waves will be named TTL_3_3 so the final suffix is the running TTL Bit.

briefdescription.para: Close a possibly open export-into-NWB file.

detaileddescription.para: We have only one NWB file open for all running devices

briefdescription.para: Check wether the given background task is running and that the device is active in multi device mode.
Calculate a cryptographic hash for the file contents of path.

parameterdescription.para: [optional, defaults to SHA-2 with 256 bytes] Type of cryptographic hash function

briefdescription.para: Check if the file paths referenced in list are pointing to identical files.
Remove all free axis from the given graph.
Remove all images from a graph.
Remove all draw layers from the graph.

memberdef.argsstring: (string graph, string trace=defaultValue, WaveOrNull wv=defaultValue, dfref dfr=defaultValue)
memberdef.name: RemoveTracesFromGraph

briefdescription.para: Remove traces from a graph and optionally try to kill their waves.

detaileddescription.para:
graph
graph
trace
[optional, default: all] remove the given trace only
wv
[optional, default: ignored] remove all traces which stem from the given wave
dfr
[optional, default: ignored] remove all traces which stem from one of the waves in dfr
Only one of trace/wv/dfr may be supplied.

briefdescription.para: Create backup waves for all waves in the datafolder.
Create a backup of the wave wv if it does not already exist or if forceCreation is true.

detaileddescription.para: The backup wave will be located in the same data folder and its name will be the original name with WAVE_BACKUP_SUFFIX appended.

briefdescription.para: Return a wave reference to the possibly not existing backup wave.
Replace all waves from the datafolder with their backup.
Replace the wave wv with its backup. If possible the backup wave will be killed afterwards.

parameterdescription.para: wave to replace by its backup
[optional, defaults to true] behaviour for the case that there is no backup. Passing a non-zero value will abort if the backup wave does not exist, with zero it will just do nothing.
[optional, defaults to false] don't delete the backup after restoring from it

simplesect.para: wave reference to the restored data, in case of no backup an invalid wave reference

briefdescription.para: Returns 1 if the user cancelled, zero if SaveExperiment was called.

detaileddescription.para: It is currently not possible to check if SaveExperiment was successfull (E-Mail from Howard Rodstein WaveMetrics, 30 Jan 2015)

parameterdescription.para: Igor symbolic path where the experiment should be stored
filename of the experiment including suffix, usually PACKED_FILE_EXPERIMENT_SUFFIX
[optional, defaults to GetInteractiveMode()] Overrides the current setting of the interactive mode

briefdescription.para: Detects duplicate values in a 1d wave.

simplesect.para: one if duplicates could be found, zero otherwise

briefdescription.para: Check that the device can act as a follower.
Check that the device is of type ITC1600.
Check that the device is a follower.
Check that the device can act as a leader.
Check that the device is a leader and has followers.
Convenience wrapper for GetFollowerList()

detaileddescription.para: For iterating over a list of all followers and the leader. Returns just device if the device can not lead.

briefdescription.para: Return a path to the program folder with trailing dir separator.

detaileddescription.para: Hardcoded as Igor does not allow to query that information.
Distinguishes between i386 and x64 Igor versions

briefdescription.para: Return the default name of a electrode.
Create a labnotebook key for unassociated channels.

detaileddescription.para: We support two types of unassociated keys. Old style, prior to 403c8ec2 (Merge pull request #370 from AllenInstitute/feature/sweepformula_enable, 2019-11-13) but after its introduction in ad8dc8ec (Allow AD/DA channels not associated with a headstage again, 2015-10-22) are written as "$Name UNASSOC_$ChannelNumber".
New style have the format "$Name u_(AD|DA)$ChannelNumber", these include the channel type to make them more self explaining.

briefdescription.para: Check if the given labnotebook entry is from an unassociated DA/AD channel.
Remove the unassociated, old and new, prefix of the given labnotebook entry name.
Start the ZeroMQ sockets and the message handler.

detaileddescription.para: Debug note: Tracking the connection state can be done via netstat | grep $port. The binded port only shows up after a successfull connection with zeromq_client_connect() is established.

memberdef.argsstring: (wave numericalValues, variable sweep, wave sweepWave, wave configWave, variable rescale, dfref targetDFR=defaultValue)
memberdef.name: SplitSweepIntoComponents

briefdescription.para: Split an DAQDataWave into one 1D-wave per channel/ttlBit.

parameterdescription.para: [optional, defaults to the sweep wave DFR] datafolder where to put the waves, can be a free datafolder

briefdescription.para: Add user data "panelVersion" to the panel.
Return 1 if the panel is up to date, zero otherwise.
Get the user data "panelVersion".

simplesect.para: numeric panel version greater 0 and -1 if no version is present or -2 if the windows does not exist

briefdescription.para: update of panel elements and related displayed graphs in BSP
Stringified short version of the clamp mode.
Stringified version of the clamp mode.
Update the repurposed sweep time global variable.

detaileddescription.para: Currently only useful for handling mid sweep analysis functions.

memberdef.argsstring: (wave numericalValues, wave textualValues, wave sweep, wave deltaI, wave deltaV, wave resistance)
memberdef.name: CalculateTPLikePropsFromSweep

briefdescription.para: Calculate deltaI/deltaV from a testpulse like stimset in "Current Clamp" mode.

xrefdescription.para: add support for evaluating "inserted TP" only

para.verbatim: embed:rst:leading-asterisk * See :ref:`CalculateTPLikePropsFromSweep_doc` for the full documentation. *

briefdescription.para: Move the source wave to the location of the given destination wave. The destination wave must be a permanent wave.

detaileddescription.para: Workaround for MoveWave having no /O flag.

parameterdescription.para: [optional, defaults to false] Overwrite referenced waves in dest with the ones from src (wave reference waves only with matching sizes)

briefdescription.para: Check if the given wave is a valid ITCConfigWave.

detaileddescription.para: The optional version parameter allows to check if the wave is at least comaptible with this version. The function assumes that higher versions are compatible with lower versions which is for most callers true. For a special case see AFH_GetChannelUnits.

parameterdescription.para: [optional, default=DAQ_CONFIG_WAVE_VERSION], check against a specific version current versions known are 0 (equals NaN), 1, 2

briefdescription.para: Check if the given wave is a valid DAQDataWave.
Check if the two waves are valid and compatible.

parameterdescription.para: [optional, defaults to DAQ_CONFIG_WAVE_VERSION] minimum required version of the config wave

briefdescription.para: Return the next random number using the device specific RNG seed.
Maps the labnotebook entry source type, one of DataAcqModes, to a valid wave index.
Rerverse the effect of EntrySourceTypeMapper()
constructs a fifo name for NI device ADC operations from the deviceID
Return the total onset delay of the given sweep.
Check if the given multiplier is a valid sampling interval multiplier.

memberdef.argsstring: (string win, string checkBoxIn, string checkBoxPartner, variable checkBoxInState)
memberdef.name: ToggleCheckBoxes

briefdescription.para: Places paired checkboxes in opposite state.

parameterdescription.para: checkbox that will be placed in opposite state

memberdef.argsstring: (string win, string checkBoxIn, string checkBoxPartner, variable checkBoxInState)
memberdef.name: EqualizeCheckBoxes

briefdescription.para: Placed paired checkboxes in same state.

parameterdescription.para: checkbox that will be placed in the same state

briefdescription.para: Return the MIES version with canonical EOLs.
Zero the wave using differentiation and integration.

detaileddescription.para: Overwrites the input wave Preserves the WaveNote and adds the entry NOTE_KEY_ZEROED
2D waves are zeroed along each row

simplesect.para: 0 if nothing was done, 1 if zeroed

briefdescription.para: Zeroes a wave in place.
Return the size of the decimated wave.

detaileddescription.para: Query that to create the output wave before calling DecimateWithMethod().

parameterdescription.para: number of rows in the input wave
decimation factor, must be an integer and larger than 1

memberdef.argsstring: (wave input, wave output, variable decimationFactor, variable method, variable firstRowInp=defaultValue, variable lastRowInp=defaultValue, variable firstColInp=defaultValue, variable lastColInp=defaultValue, variable firstColOut=defaultValue, variable lastColOut=defaultValue, WaveOrNull factor=defaultValue)
memberdef.name: DecimateWithMethod

briefdescription.para: Decimate the the given input wave.

detaileddescription.para: This allows to decimate a given input row range into output rows using the given method. The columns of input/output can be different. The input row coordinates can be used to do a chunked conversion, e.g. when receiving data from hardware. Incomplete chunks will be redone when necessary.
Algorithm visualized:

para.verbatim: embed:rst:leading-asterisk * .. code-block:: text * * Input (16 entries): [ | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | ] * Decimation factor: 4 * Method: MinMax * Output (4 entries): [ min(input[0, 7]) | max(input[0, 7]) | min(input[8, 15]) | max(input[8, 15]) ] * *

parameterdescription.para: target wave which will be around decimationFactor smaller than input
decimation factor, must be an integer and larger than 1
[optional, defaults to 0] first row input coordinates
[optional, defaults to last element] last row in input coordinates
[optional, defaults to 0] first col in input coordinates
[optional, defaults to last element] last col in input coordinates
[optional, defaults to firstColInp] first col in output coordinates
[optional, defaults to lastColInp] last col in output coordinates
[optional, defaults to none] factor which is applied to all input columns and written into the output columns

memberdef.argsstring: (wave input, wave output, variable idx, variable firstRowInp, variable lastRowInp, variable colInp, variable colOut, variable decimationFactor)
memberdef.name: DecimateMinMax

briefdescription.para: Threadsafe helper function for DecimateWithMethod.
Starts with a new experiment.

detaileddescription.para: You have to manually save before, see SaveExperimentWrapper()

briefdescription.para: Remove the volatile part of the XOP error code.

detaileddescription.para: The result is constant and can therefore be compared with constants.
From http://www.igorexchange.com/node/7286

memberdef.argsstring: (wave numericOrTextWave, variable col=defaultValue, string colLabel=defaultValue, variable var=defaultValue, string str=defaultValue, variable prop=defaultValue, variable startRow=defaultValue, variable endRow=defaultValue, variable startLayer=defaultValue, variable endLayer=defaultValue)
memberdef.name: FindIndizes

briefdescription.para: Extended version of FindValue

detaileddescription.para: Allows to search only the specified column for a value and returns all matching row indizes in a wave. By defaults only looks into the first layer for backward compatibility reasons. When multiple layers are searched startLayer/endLayer the result contains matches from all layers, and this also means the resulting wave is still 1D.
Exactly one of var/str/prop has to be given except for prop == PROP_MATCHES_VAR_BIT_MASK and prop == PROP_NOT_MATCHES_VAR_BIT_MASK which requires a var/str parameter as well.
Exactly one of col/colLabel has to be given.

parameterdescription.para: [optional] column label to search in only
[optional] starting row to restrict the search to
[optional] ending row to restrict the search to
[optional, defaults to zero] starting layer to restrict search to
[optional, defaults to zero] ending layer to restrict search to

simplesect.para: A wave with the row indizes of the found values. An invalid wave reference if the value could not be found.

briefdescription.para: Searches the column colLabel in wv for an non-empty entry with a row number smaller or equal to endRow.

detaileddescription.para: Return an empty string if nothing could be found.

briefdescription.para: Generate a default settings file in JSON format.

detaileddescription.para: Explanation:
Window coordinates are stored as
Entries:

listitem.para: "version": Major version number to track breaking changes
"diagnostics": Groups settings related to diagnostics and crash dump handling
"diagnostics/last upload": ISO8601 timestamp when the last successfull upload of crash dumps was tried. This is also set when no crash dumps have been uploadad.
"analysisbrowser": Groups settings related to the Analysisbrowser
"analysisbrowser/directory": The directory initially opened for browsing existing NWB/PXP files
"logfiles": Groups settings related to log files
"logfiles/last upload": ISO8601 timestamp when the last successfull upload of log files was tried. This is also set when no log files have been uploadad.

detaileddescription.para:
JSONid
Caller is responsible for releasing the document.

briefdescription.para: Call UploadCrashDumps() if we haven't called it since at least a day.
Call UploadLogFiles() if we haven't called it since at least a day.
Return the graph user data as 2D text wave.

detaileddescription.para:
Only returns infos for sweep traces without duplicates. Duplicates are present with oodDAQ display mode.
graph
Name of graph
addFilterKeys
[optional, default = $""] additional keys for filtering
addFilterValues
[optional, default = $""] additional values for filtering, must have same size as keys

briefdescription.para: Remove the given sweep from the Databrowser/Sweepbrowser.

detaileddescription.para: Needs a manual call to PostPlotTransformations() afterwards.

briefdescription.para: Add the given sweep to the Databrowser/Sweepbrowser.

detaileddescription.para: Needs a manual call to PostPlotTransformations() afterwards.

parameterdescription.para: [optional, default = n/a] BufferedDrawInfo structure, when given buffered draw is used.

briefdescription.para: Update the given sweep in the Databrowser/Sweepbrowser plot.

detaileddescription.para: Needs a manual call to PostPlotTransformations() afterwards.

briefdescription.para: Generic window hooks for storing the window coordinates in the JSON settings file.
Map from analysis function name to numeric constant.

simplesect.para: One of SpecialAnalysisFunctionTypes which includes INVALID_ANALYSIS_FUNCTION

memberdef.argsstring: (variable type, string formatString, variable chunk=defaultValue, variable query=defaultValue)
memberdef.name: CreateAnaFuncLBNKey

briefdescription.para: Return labnotebook keys for patch seq analysis functions.

parameterdescription.para: [optional] Some format strings expect a chunk number
[optional, defaults to false] If the key is to be used for setting or querying the labnotebook

briefdescription.para: Return the current version of the analysis functions.

memberdef.argsstring: (string device, variable type, variable headstage, variable sweepNo)
memberdef.name: SetAnalysisFunctionVersion

briefdescription.para: Add a labnotebook entry denoting the analysis function version.
Return JSON text with default entries for upload.

detaileddescription.para: Caller is responsible for releasing JSON text.

briefdescription.para: Convert the Igor Pro crash dumps and the report file to JSON and upload them.

detaileddescription.para: Does nothing if none of these files exists.
The uploaded files are moved out of the way afterwards.
See tools/http-upload/upload-json-payload-v1.php for the JSON format description.

simplesect.para: 1 if crash dumps had been uploaded, 0 otherwise

briefdescription.para: Upload the MIES and ZeroMQ logfiles.

parameterdescription.para: [optional, defaults to true] Only in verbose mode the ticket ID is output to the history

[optional, defaults to false] Allows to filter the logfiles to include entries within the given dates. Both firstDate and lastDate must be present for filtering. The timestamps are in seconds since Igor Pro epoch.

briefdescription.para: Filter the entries text wave so that the result only includes entries between first and last.

parameterdescription.para: 1D wave with a JSON document in each entry. Each JSON must contain a ts object with a ISO8601 timestamp.
Seconds since igor epoch in UTC of the first entry to include
Seconds since igor epoch in UTC of the last entry to include

briefdescription.para: Update the logging template used by the ZeroMQ-XOP.
Return the disc location of the (possibly non-existing) ZeroMQ-XOP logfile.
Tries to find deleted sweep and config waves which are still present due to 1D waves from databrowser backups or labnotebook entries.

detaileddescription.para: The result is placed in root:reconstructed which is overwritten.
For reconstructing single sweep or config waves, see RecreateSweepWaveFromBackupAndLBN() and RecreateConfigWaveFromLBN().

Example invocation:
RecreateMissingSweepAndConfigWaves("ITC18USB_DEV_0", root:MIES:HardwareDevices:ITC18USB:Device0:Data:)
Inspect the results in root:reconstructed
If you are satisfied, move the waves from root:reconstructed into e.g. root:MIES:HardwareDevices:ITC18USB:Device0:Data:
Proceed with the data as usual
In case the routine throws an assertion, please open an issue so that we can investigate.

memberdef.argsstring: (wave numericalValues, WaveText textualValues, variable sweepNo, dfref deviceDataDFR)
memberdef.name: RecreateSweepWaveFromBackupAndLBN

briefdescription.para: Try recreating a 2D DAQ sweep wave from its databrowser backup.

simplesect.para: $"" if reconstruction failed or a free wave with the sweep data and correct metadata

memberdef.argsstring: (string device, wave numericalValues, wave textualValues, variable sweepNo)
memberdef.name: RecreateConfigWaveFromLBN

briefdescription.para: Try recreating the DAQ config wave from labnotebook entries.

simplesect.para: $"" if recreation failed or a free wave on success.

memberdef.argsstring: (wave numericalValues, wave textualValues, variable sweepNo, wave configWave, variable channelType)
memberdef.name: AddChannelPropertiesFromLBN

briefdescription.para: Set ChannelNumber and ChannelType in configWave.

memberdef.argsstring: (wave numericalValues, wave textualValues, variable sweepNo, wave configWave)
memberdef.name: AddDAQChannelTypeFromLBN

briefdescription.para: Set DAQChannelType in configWave.

memberdef.argsstring: (wave numericalValues, WaveText textualValues, variable sweepNo, wave configWave)
memberdef.name: AddChannelUnitFromLBN

briefdescription.para: Add wave note entry CHANNEL_UNIT_KEY for the used channel units.
Return waves with the channel numbers and references to 1D waves from the X_XXXX folders.
Add the returned 1D waves from GetSingleSweepWaves() into sweepWave.
Return if the function results are overriden for testing purposes.

memberdef.argsstring: (wave numericalValues, variable sweep, variable channelType, variable channelNumber, variable entrySourceType)
memberdef.name: GetHeadstageForChannel

briefdescription.para: Returns the active headstage for a given sweep, channelType, channelNumber from the LBN.

parameterdescription.para: type of the labnotebook entry, one of DataAcqModes

simplesect.para: headstage number or NaN if no headstage was found

briefdescription.para: This file holds utility functions which need to know about MIES internals.
